title: Why Intentional Relationships Are the Ultimate Business Strategy
guestBio: Scott MacGregor is an entrepreneur, publisher, and 4x author with 20+ years in sales leadership. As founder of The Outlier Project and Editor-in-Chief of Outlier Magazine, he champions authentic relationship building over traditional networking.
excerpt: Scott MacGregor reveals why intentional relationship building is the one skill AI can never replace. Learn his 'extra inch' philosophy and why genuine human connection trumps every business strategy.
metaDescription: Learn why intentional relationship building beats every business strategy. Scott MacGregor shares his proven methods for authentic connections.
keyTakeaways: Stop trying to figure out the end result and just pour into people with genuine intention.
Going the 'extra inch' with 5 seconds of additional effort creates 100x the impact in relationships.
Use your phone's contact notes as a simple but powerful CRM to track meaningful details about people.
Shift from networking to friendship building by genuinely caring about people as humans first.
Relationships follow unpredictable paths, so trust the process rather than forcing outcomes.
Choose impact over income because people remember how you made them feel, not your revenue.
Daily relationship building, like athletic training, strengthens your connection muscles over time.

pullQuotes.text: The future belongs to the most connected

timestamps.label: Why relationships are the most valuable currency
The power of going the extra inch
The birthday video that changed everything
Using your phone as a personal CRM

content:
In a world obsessed with automation and efficiency, Scott MacGregor is making a radical claim: the most valuable business skill isn't something you can optimize, systematize, or delegate to artificial intelligence. It's the deeply human art of intentional relationship building.

As the founder of The Outlier Project and publisher of Outlier Magazine, Scott has built a thriving business empire not through clever marketing tactics or growth hacks, but through what he calls going the extra inch. This isn't about networking in the traditional sense—it's about genuinely caring for people in ways that create lasting, meaningful connections.

The Extra Inch That Changes Everything

Scott's philosophy centers on a simple but powerful concept: most people go the extra mile when it suits them, but very few consistently go the extra inch in their daily interactions. This extra inch might look like recording a personal video message on someone's birthday while you're on the treadmill at 5am, or taking thirty seconds to add meaningful details about someone to your phone contacts after meeting them.

The magic isn't in the grand gestures—it's in the consistent, small acts of intentionality that show people they matter to you. When Scott sends that slightly sweaty, unpolished birthday video from his morning workout, he's not trying to impress anyone. He's choosing genuine connection over perfect presentation, and that authenticity resonates in ways that polished corporate messages never could.

Your Phone as the Ultimate Relationship Tool

One of Scott's most practical strategies involves transforming your smartphone into a personal relationship management system. Instead of just storing names and numbers, he tracks meaningful details about the people in his life. When someone mentions their daughter's soccer tournament or their upcoming work presentation, those details go into his contact notes.

This isn't about manipulation or calculated networking—it's about caring enough to remember. When you genuinely care about people, you naturally want to remember what matters to them. The phone becomes simply a tool to support that caring intention, not replace it.

From Networking to Friendship Building

The shift Scott advocates requires abandoning traditional networking mindsets entirely. Instead of asking What can this person do for me? or How can I impress them?, the question becomes How can I genuinely support this human being?

This mindset transformation changes everything about how you show up in professional and personal relationships. When you approach connections with authentic care rather than strategic calculation, people feel the difference immediately. They sense that you see them as a whole person, not just a potential business opportunity.

The Warren Buffett Approach to Human Connection

Scott draws parallels between his relationship philosophy and Warren Buffett's investment strategy: slow, steady, and focused on long-term value rather than quick returns. Just as Buffett doesn't try to time the market or chase trending stocks, effective relationship builders don't try to force immediate outcomes from their connections.

The relationships that transform your business and life often develop along circuitous, unpredictable paths. That colleague you helped five years ago might become your biggest advocate when you least expect it. The person you supported through a difficult time might open doors you never knew existed.

Why AI Makes Human Connection More Valuable

As artificial intelligence handles more transactional aspects of business, the premium on genuine human connection only increases. AI can analyze data, automate processes, and even generate content, but it cannot replace the irreplaceable human elements of empathy, intuition, and authentic care.

Scott predicts that leaders who master intentional relationship building will have an increasingly significant advantage as the business world becomes more automated. While others rely on systems and processes, these leaders will thrive through the irreplaceable power of human connection.

Impact Over Income: The Sustainable Path

Perhaps most importantly, Scott's approach prioritizes impact over income, meaning over money. He deliberately caps The Outlier Project's membership because he believes that scale can steal the soul from meaningful work. This isn't about limiting success—it's about defining success differently.

When you focus on making genuine impact in people's lives rather than maximizing revenue streams, you create something sustainable and fulfilling. People remember how you made them feel long after they've forgotten your business metrics or quarterly reports.

The future belongs to those who understand that in an increasingly connected yet lonely world, the ability to forge genuine human connections isn't just a nice-to-have skill—it's the ultimate competitive advantage. Scott MacGregor's approach offers a roadmap for building not just a successful business, but a meaningful life filled with authentic relationships that enrich everyone involved.
